arxiv.title: Evo-MedAgent: Beyond One-Shot Diagnosis with Agents That Remember, Reflect, and Improve
arxiv.abstract: Tool-augmented large language model (LLM) agents can orchestrate specialist classifiers, segmentation models, and visual question-answering modules to interpret chest X-rays. arxiv.title: Filling in the Mechanisms: How do LMs Learn Filler-Gap Dependencies under Developmental Constraints?
arxiv.abstract: For humans, filler-gap dependencies require a shared representation across different syntactic constructions. Although causal analyses suggest this may also be true for LLMs (Boguraev et al., 2025), it is still unclear if such a representation also exists for language models trained on developmental

arxiv.title: AIBuildAI: An AI Agent for Automatically Building AI Models
arxiv.abstract: AI models underpin modern intelligent systems, driving advances across science, medicine, finance, and technology. arxiv.title: Exascale Multi-Task Graph Foundation Models for Imbalanced, Multi-Fidelity Atomistic Data
arxiv.abstract: We present an exascale workflow for materials discovery using atomistic graph foundation models built on HydraGNN. We jointly train on 16 open first-principles datasets (544+ million structures covering 85+ elements) using a multi-task architecture with per-dataset heads and a scalable ADIOS2/DDStor

arxiv.title: Seeing Through Circuits: Faithful Mechanistic Interpretability for Vision Transformers
arxiv.abstract: Transparency of neural networks' internal reasoning is at the heart of interpretability research, adding to trust, safety, and understanding of these models. arxiv.title: Scouting By Reward: VLM-TO-IRL-Driven Player Selection For Esports
arxiv.abstract: Traditional esports scouting workflows rely heavily on manual video review and aggregate performance metrics, which often fail to capture the nuanced decision-making patterns necessary to determine if a prospect fits a specific tactical archetype. arxiv.title: Psychological Steering of Large Language Models
arxiv.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) emulate a consistent human-like behavior that can be shaped through activation-level interventions. arxiv.title: MARCA: A Checklist-Based Benchmark for Multilingual Web Search
arxiv.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as sources of information, yet their reliability depends on the ability to search the web, select relevant evidence, and synthesize complete answers. arxiv.title: Hierarchical vs. Flat Iteration in Shared-Weight Transformers
arxiv.abstract: We present an empirical study of whether hierarchically structured, shared-weight recurrence can match the representational quality of independent-layer stacking in a Transformer-based language model. arxiv.title: LLMs taking shortcuts in test generation: A study with SAP HANA and LevelDB
arxiv.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved impressive results on public benchmarks, often leading to claims of advanced reasoning and understanding.
